title: Property Tax Information * Older Adults - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Property Tax Information * Older Adults 1 Matching Service Providers Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF REVENUE SENIOR AND DISABLED PROPERTY TAX DEFERRAL PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Qualified senior and disabled citizens can "borrow" from the State of Oregon to pay property taxes to the county. If approved, the Oregon Department of Revenue (DOR) will pay county property taxes on November 15 of each year. A lien is placed on the property and DOR becomes a security interest holder. 955 Center Street NE Salem, OR 973012555 Eligibility: Must be either 62 or older, or must be a disabled citizen who is receiving or is eligible to receive federal Social Security disability benefits. Also, must have a recorded deed to the property or be buying the property under a recorded sales contract. Certain trust or trustee arrangements qualify for deferral. Not eligible for deferral if person has a life estate interest in the property. See application for more eligibility requirements. Hours: File an application between January 1 and April 15 to defer the taxes due the following November 15 Email (503) 945-8348 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF REVENUE SENIOR AND DISABLED PROPERTY TAX DEFERRAL PROGRAM
